steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Critics use strongly positive language — "galvanizing" and "picture perfect" — to describe the two films. Fans hope that such ______ will attract new audiences. The blank must name the enthusiastic critical approval that the critics' words express.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'ambivalence': means mixed or uncertain feelings — the critics express unambiguously positive judgment, so this directly contradicts the tone of "galvanizing" and "picture perfect." ✗
  • Choice B — 'acclaim': means enthusiastic public praise or critical approval — this precisely captures what critics bestow when they call a film "galvanizing" and "picture perfect." ✓
  • Choice C — 'foresight': means the ability to predict future events — the critics are evaluating existing films, not predicting outcomes, so this is unrelated to the critics' act of reviewing. ✗
  • Choice D — 'impartiality': means neutrality or lack of bias — critics who call a film "galvanizing" and "picture perfect" are taking a strong positive stance, not a neutral one. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
